name: Markdown

values.name: customHtml
values.comment:
Customize how to render each element. The following examples
demonstrate how to use it.

- Render `target="_blank"` on links depending on the url.
[Demo](https://pablohirafuji.github.io/elm-markdown/examples/CustomLinkTag.html)
/ [Code](https://github.com/pablohirafuji/elm-markdown/blob/master/examples/CustomLinkTag.elm)
- Render images using `figure` and `figcaption` elements.
[Demo](https://pablohirafuji.github.io/elm-markdown/examples/CustomImageTag.html)
/ [Code](https://github.com/pablohirafuji/elm-markdown/blob/master/examples/CustomImageTag.elm)

values.name: toHtml
values.comment:
Turn a markdown string into a list of HTML elements,
using the `Config.defaultOptions` and `Config.defaultElements`.

```

type Msg
    = MsgOfmyApp1
    | MsgOfmyApp2
    | MsgOfmyApp3
    | Markdown


markdownView : Html Msg
markdownView =
    Html.map (always Markdown)
        <| section []
        <| Markdown.toHtml "# Title with *emphasis*"

```

values.name: withOptions
values.comment:
Customize how soft line breaks (`\n`) are rendered and raw html
tags are parsed.

```
customOptions : Options
customOptions =
    { softAsHardLineBreak = True
    , rawHtml = DontParse
    }


view : Html Never
view =
    div []
        <| Markdown.withOptions customOptions model.textarea
```

The [demo](https://pablohirafuji.github.io/elm-markdown/examples/Demo.html)
demonstrate how each option affects the output.

name: Markdown.Config

unions.name: HtmlOption
unions.comment:
Choose what to do with raw html elements.

- `ParseUnsafe`: Will parse any valid html tag and attribute. This includes malicious code like `<script>alert("XSS ALERT!!");</script>`.
- `Sanitize SanitizeOptions`: Will parse only specific html elements and attributes.
- `DontParse`: Do not parse any html tag. It will include the raw text in the output.

unions.name: ListElement
unions.comment:
List element model. The `Int` in `Ordered Int` is the
first number used to start an ordered list.

aliases.name: CodeBlock
aliases.comment:
Code block element model. If you want to use a highlight package,
you have access to the code here.

aliases.name: Elements
aliases.comment:
A record to be used with `customHtml` of how each element
is rendered to html.
aliases.type: { heading : Int -> List (Html Never) -> Html Never, thematicBreak : Html Never, paragraph : Bool -> List (Html Never) -> List (Html Never), blockQuote : List (Html Never) -> Html Never, code : CodeBlock -> Html Never, list : ListElement -> List (Html Never) -> Html Never, emphasis : List (Html Never) -> Html Never, strongEmphasis : List (Html Never) -> Html Never, codeSpan : String -> Html Never, link : Link -> List (Html Never) -> Html Never, image : Image -> Html Never, hardLineBreak : Html Never }

aliases.name: Image
aliases.type: { alt : String, src : String, title : Maybe String }

aliases.name: Options
aliases.comment:
Some parser options so you can tweak things for your particular case. - `softAsHardLineBreak`: If set to `True`, will render `\n` as `
`. - `rawHtml`: This determine what to do with raw html elements.

aliases.name: SanitizeOptions
aliases.comment:
Choose what html elements and attributes are allowed to parse.

- `allowedHtmlElements`: List of allowed html elements.
- `allowedHtmlAttributes`: List of allowed attributes.
aliases.type: { allowedHtmlElements : List String, allowedHtmlAttributes : List String }

values.name: defaultOptions
values.comment:
The `Options` used in `Markdown.toHtml`.

```
{ softAsHardLineBreak = False
, rawHtml = Sanitize defaultSanitizeOptions
}
```
